description: Pipelined v4 — Kanban board view, AI resume-job fit scoring, command palette, onboarding flow, keyboard shortcuts, application goals, company logos, follow-up reminders, contacts tracker, notification center, activity feed, and auto-save extension mode.

userStories.title: Kanban board view with drag-and-drop stage transitions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view my applications as a Kanban board where each column is a pipeline stage and each card is an application, so I can drag applications between stages visually. This is the most natural UX for a pipeline tracker.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @dnd-kit/core and @dnd-kit/sortable as dependencies (npm install @dnd-kit/core @dnd-kit/sortable)
Create frontend/src/components/KanbanBoard.jsx: renders one column per stage from the user's default_stages (via useAuth hook), each column has a header with stage name + count badge

Create frontend/src/components/KanbanCard.jsx: a draggable card showing company name (bold), role_title, formatRelative(date_applied) from dateUtils, and a stale amber dot if applicable — card has rounded corners, subtle shadow, and dark mode support
KanbanBoard uses DndContext + SortableContext from @dnd-kit; dropping a card on a different column calls useUpdateApplication mutate({ current_stage: newStage })
Each column scrolls vertically independently (max-height with overflow-y-auto), empty columns show a dashed placeholder 'No applications'

Dashboard.jsx adds a view toggle in the header: two icon buttons (list icon and grid/kanban icon) that switch between ApplicationList and KanbanBoard — persist the choice to localStorage key 'pipelined_view_mode' (default: 'list')
KanbanBoard respects all active filters from FilterBar (stage, company_type, remote_status, search query) — it receives the same filtered data that ApplicationList receives
Clicking a KanbanCard sets the URL param ?selected={id} to open DetailPanel, same as clicking a list row
On mobile (< md breakpoint), KanbanBoard renders columns as horizontal scrollable tabs instead of side-by-side columns
Vitest test for KanbanBoard: renders correct number of columns matching user's default_stages, renders cards in correct columns by current_stage
Vitest test for KanbanCard: renders company and role title, applies stale indicator class when application is stale
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This is the #1 most requested UX pattern for pipeline trackers. Every competitor (Huntr, Teal, Simplify) has Kanban. The table-only view is a dealbreaker for visual users. Use the user's custom stages from default_stages (US-068) as columns. If US-068 hasn't landed yet, fall back to the hardcoded STAGES constant from constants.js.

userStories.title: AI resume-job fit scoring on application save
userStories.description: As a user, I want to upload my resume once and see an AI-generated fit score for each application, showing which skills match and which are missing. This transforms Pipelined from a spreadsheet into an intelligent career tool.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend: add pdfplumber to requirements.txt (pip package for PDF text extraction)

Backend: POST /api/auth/resume accepts multipart/form-data with a PDF file (max 2MB), extracts text via pdfplumber, stores it as resume_text (string) on the user document, returns 200 { data: { chars_extracted: int } }
Backend: DELETE /api/auth/resume clears resume_text from the user document, returns 204
Backend: GET /api/auth/me includes has_resume: bool (true if resume_text is non-empty) in the response — do NOT return the full resume_text in the response for bandwidth

Backend: create backend/parsing/fit_scorer.py with async function score_fit(resume_text: str, job_description: str) -> dict that calls OpenAI GPT-4o mini with a prompt returning JSON: { fit_score: int (0-100), matched_skills: list[str] (max 8), missing_skills: list[str] (max 5), summary: str (one sentence) }
fit_scorer.py uses temperature=0, max_tokens=300, timeout=8 seconds; on any error returns { fit_score: None, matched_skills: [], missing_skills: [], summary: None }

Backend: in applications/service.py create_application(), after successful insert, if the user has resume_text and the application has source='extension' with page_text OR has role_title+company, fire-and-forget an asyncio.create_task() that calls score_fit() and updates the application document with ai_analysis: { fit_score, matched_skills, missing_skills, summary, scored_at }
Backend: GET /api/applications/{id} response includes ai_analysis field (null if not scored)
Backend: GET /api/applications list response includes ai_analysis.fit_score in the projection (just the score number for list view, not full analysis)

Frontend: create frontend/src/components/FitBadge.jsx: a small colored pill showing fit_score — green (80-100), yellow (50-79), orange (30-49), red (0-29), gray if null/pending; shows 'AI' label + score number
Frontend: ApplicationList.jsx renders FitBadge in each row after the stage column (only if score is not null)
Frontend: KanbanCard.jsx (from US-078) renders FitBadge in bottom-right corner of card if score exists

Frontend: DetailPanel.jsx shows a 'Resume Fit' section when ai_analysis is present: fit_score as a large number with color, matched_skills as green chips, missing_skills as red chips, summary text below

Frontend: Settings page (or a new section in the NavBar user menu) has 'Upload Resume' button — file input accepting .pdf, calls POST /api/auth/resume, shows success/error toast, and a 'Remove Resume' button if has_resume is true
Backend test: test_fit_scorer.py mocks OpenAI and verifies score_fit returns correct shape; test error fallback returns nulls
Backend test: test_auth.py adds test for POST /api/auth/resume with a valid PDF fixture, verifies has_resume=true in GET /me
Vitest test for FitBadge: renders correct color class for each score range, renders gray for null
All existing tests pass
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This leverages the existing OpenAI GPT-4o mini integration. The scoring is fire-and-forget so it doesn't slow down application creation. The fit_score is stored on the application document for fast retrieval. Budget guard: if OPENAI_API_KEY is empty, skip scoring silently. pdfplumber is a pure-Python PDF extractor, no system dependencies.

userStories.title: Command palette with Cmd+K global search and actions
userStories.description: As a power user, I want to press Cmd+K (or Ctrl+K) anywhere in the app to open a search/action palette where I can instantly find applications, navigate to pages, or trigger actions — like VS Code, Linear, and Notion.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/components/CommandPalette.jsx: a modal overlay that opens on Cmd+K (Mac) / Ctrl+K (Windows/Linux), with a search input at the top and a scrollable results list below
The palette has three sections separated by headers: 'Applications' (search results), 'Navigation' (page links), 'Actions' (quick actions)

Typing in the search input filters applications by company/role name (client-side filter against cached React Query data from useApplications, no extra API call for first 100 apps; if >100 apps, debounce 200ms and call the API with q= param)
Application results show: company name (bold), role title, stage badge — clicking navigates to /dashboard?selected={id}
Navigation items (always shown when search is empty): 'Dashboard' → /dashboard, 'Calendar' → /calendar, 'Analytics' → /analytics, 'Job Board' → /jobs, 'Settings' → /settings
Action items (always shown when search is empty): 'Add Application' (opens ManualAddForm), 'Export CSV' (triggers export), 'Toggle Dark Mode' (calls setTheme from ThemeContext)
Arrow keys (up/down) navigate the result list with visual highlight; Enter activates the highlighted item; Escape closes the palette
The palette renders a semi-transparent backdrop that closes on click outside
Palette is mounted once in App.jsx (not re-mounted per page) and listens for the keyboard shortcut globally via useEffect on document
Results show a keyboard shortcut hint next to navigation items (e.g., '1' next to Dashboard) for discoverability of future keyboard shortcuts
Supports dark mode (dark: variants on all surfaces)
Vitest test for CommandPalette: opens on Cmd+K keydown, search filters results, arrow keys change highlighted index, Enter triggers navigation, Escape closes
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Do NOT add a third-party command palette library (cmdk, kbar) — implement it as a focused component to keep bundle size small. The palette is a thin wrapper: input + filtered list + keyboard navigation. Total should be under 200 lines.

userStories.title: Onboarding flow and rich empty states for new users
userStories.description: As a first-time user, when I sign up and land on an empty dashboard, I have no idea what to do. I need guided onboarding and helpful empty states that teach me how to use Pipelined and encourage me to add my first application.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/components/OnboardingChecklist.jsx: a dismissable card shown at the top of Dashboard when the user has 0 applications AND localStorage key 'pipelined_onboarding_dismissed' is not 'true'

The checklist shows 3 steps with checkboxes: (1) 'Install the Chrome extension' — links to chrome://extensions or a placeholder URL, checked if user has any source='extension' applications, (2) 'Add your first application' — button that opens ManualAddForm, checked if total applications > 0, (3) 'Customize your pipeline stages' — links to /settings, checked if user has modified default_stages (i.e., default_stages differs from the system default list)
Each unchecked step has a brief description (one sentence) explaining why it matters
A 'Dismiss' text button in the top-right of the checklist sets localStorage and hides it permanently
When all 3 steps are checked, the checklist auto-hides with a confetti-like success message ('You are all set!') and auto-dismisses after 3 seconds
Create frontend/src/components/EmptyState.jsx: a reusable empty state component accepting title, description, icon (Lucide icon component), and optional actionButton (label + onClick) props

Dashboard (ApplicationList area): when data is empty and not loading, render EmptyState with title 'No applications yet', description 'Start tracking your job search by adding your first application.', icon FolderOpen, actions: 'Add Application' button + 'Import CSV' button (if US-069 import exists, otherwise just Add)

Calendar page: when no events exist, render EmptyState with title 'No interviews scheduled', description 'Events will appear here when you schedule interviews for your applications.', icon CalendarDays
Analytics page: when total applications < 3, render EmptyState with title 'Not enough data yet', description 'Add at least 3 applications to unlock analytics and insights.', icon BarChart3
All empty states support dark mode
Vitest test for OnboardingChecklist: renders 3 steps, dismiss button hides checklist, step is checked when condition is met
Vitest test for EmptyState: renders title, description, and action button; clicking action button fires callback
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: First-session retention is everything for a new app. An empty dashboard is a dead end. The onboarding checklist is inspired by Linear's setup flow. The EmptyState component is reusable across every page. Do not over-design — keep it clean, minimal, and actionable.

userStories.title: Global keyboard shortcuts with cheat sheet modal
userStories.description: As a keyboard-driven user (most CS students), I want vim-like shortcuts throughout the app so I can navigate and manage applications without touching my mouse.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/hooks/useHotkeys.js: a custom hook that registers keyboard shortcuts — useHotkeys(key: string, callback: () => void, options?: { enabled?: boolean }) — listens on document keydown, ignores events when focus is in an input/textarea/select or contentEditable element
Create frontend/src/lib/shortcuts.js: exports a SHORTCUTS constant array of { key, label, description, scope } objects for all shortcuts (used by both the hook registrations and the cheat sheet)

Dashboard shortcuts (active when on /dashboard): 'j' moves selection down one row in ApplicationList, 'k' moves selection up one row, 'Enter' opens DetailPanel for selected row, 'a' opens ManualAddForm, 'x' toggles selection checkbox on current row, 'Escape' closes DetailPanel or deselects

Global shortcuts (active on all pages): 'g then d' (chord) navigates to /dashboard, 'g then c' to /calendar, 'g then a' to /analytics, 'g then j' to /jobs, '?' opens the keyboard shortcuts cheat sheet modal, 'Cmd+K' or 'Ctrl+K' opens CommandPalette (from US-080 — if not yet built, wire the shortcut but no-op)
DetailPanel shortcuts (active when DetailPanel is open): 's' focuses the stage dropdown, 'n' focuses the notes textarea, 'Escape' closes the panel

Create frontend/src/components/ShortcutHelp.jsx: a modal showing all shortcuts organized by scope (Global, Dashboard, Detail Panel) in a clean two-column layout (key on left, description on right) — opens when '?' is pressed
Keyboard shortcut hints shown in tooltips on major action buttons (e.g., ManualAddForm trigger button tooltip shows 'Add Application (A)')
All shortcuts are disabled when any modal, form, or command palette is open (check for open overlay state)
Supports dark mode
Vitest test for useHotkeys: fires callback on correct keydown, does NOT fire when input is focused, respects enabled option
Vitest test for ShortcutHelp: renders all shortcut groups, opens on '?' keydown
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Chord shortcuts ('g then d') use a 500ms timeout window — press 'g', then within 500ms press 'd'. If timeout expires, reset. This is the vim/Gmail pattern. The useHotkeys hook should be lightweight (<50 lines) — do NOT use a third-party hotkey library.

userStories.title: Weekly application goals with streak tracker and progress ring
userStories.description: As a job seeker, I want to set a weekly application goal and see my progress visually, with a streak counter for consecutive weeks I hit my target. Gamification that keeps me accountable during the job search grind.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend: PATCH /api/auth/me accepts { weekly_goal: int } (validated: ge=1, le=50, default=5); stored on user document
Backend: GET /api/auth/me returns weekly_goal in response (default 5 if not set)

Backend: GET /api/applications/stats response adds two new fields: applied_this_week: int (applications where date_applied is within current ISO week Mon-Sun) and current_streak: int (consecutive past weeks where application count >= weekly_goal, looking back up to 52 weeks)
The streak calculation uses a single aggregation pipeline: group applications by ISO week, count per week, iterate backwards from last complete week to find consecutive weeks meeting the goal

Frontend: create frontend/src/components/GoalProgress.jsx: a compact card showing (1) a circular SVG progress ring (applied_this_week / weekly_goal as percentage, animated fill), (2) '{applied_this_week} / {weekly_goal} this week' text, (3) a flame icon + '{current_streak} week streak' below (only if streak > 0)
GoalProgress.jsx renders in the StatsBar area (as a 5th stat card) or as a standalone card above StatsBar on Dashboard
The progress ring is green when goal is met (100%+), blue when in progress, gray when 0
Settings page adds a 'Weekly Application Goal' number input (1-50) that calls PATCH /api/auth/me
When applied_this_week reaches weekly_goal for the first time in a session, show a celebratory toast: 'Goal reached! {applied_this_week} applications this week'
GoalProgress supports dark mode
Backend test: test_applications.py adds test for applied_this_week and current_streak in stats response (create applications with specific date_applied values to verify weekly grouping)
Vitest test for GoalProgress: renders progress ring with correct percentage, shows streak when > 0, hides streak when 0
All existing tests pass
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: The SVG progress ring is a simple circle with stroke-dasharray/stroke-dashoffset — no charting library needed, about 20 lines of SVG. Streak calculation should handle edge cases: first week of use (streak = 0 until a full week completes), weeks with 0 applications break the streak. ISO weeks start on Monday.

userStories.title: Company logo enrichment via Clearbit favicon API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see company logos next to my applications so the dashboard and Kanban board feel polished and professional instead of text-only walls.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend: in applications/service.py, after creating an application, if source_url is present, extract the domain (urlparse(source_url).netloc, strip 'www.' prefix) and store it as company_domain on the application document

Backend: if source_url is absent but company is present, derive a best-guess domain: company.lower().replace(' ', '').replace(',', '').replace('.', '') + '.com' — store as company_domain (heuristic, not guaranteed correct)
Backend: GET /api/applications list projection includes company_domain field

Frontend: create frontend/src/components/CompanyLogo.jsx: accepts company_domain (string | null) and company (string) props; renders an tag with src='https://logo.clearbit.com/{company_domain}' (size 32x32), with an onError fallback that hides the image and shows a colored circle with the first letter of the company name (like Gmail avatar fallbacks)
The fallback letter circle uses a deterministic color based on company name hash (pick from 8 muted colors) so the same company always gets the same color
ApplicationList.jsx: render CompanyLogo at the start of each row (before company name), size 24x24
KanbanCard.jsx (US-078): render CompanyLogo in the top-left of the card, size 20x20
DetailPanel.jsx: render CompanyLogo next to the company name in the header, size 32x32
CompanyLogo img tags include loading='lazy' for performance (many logos on screen at once)
CompanyLogo supports dark mode (fallback circle has dark: variants)
Vitest test for CompanyLogo: renders img with correct src for valid domain, renders fallback letter on img error, fallback shows correct first letter
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Clearbit Logo API (logo.clearbit.com) is free, requires no API key, and returns company logos by domain. It's used by Notion, Linear, and most SaaS apps. The letter fallback ensures every application has a visual anchor even when the logo fails. Do NOT cache logos on the backend — let the browser cache handle it (Clearbit sets long Cache-Control headers).

userStories.title: Application follow-up reminders with due date tracking
userStories.description: As a job seeker, following up on applications is the #1 thing I forget to do. I want to set a 'follow up by' date on any application, and see overdue follow-ups prominently on my dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend: application document schema adds follow_up_date: datetime | None = None
Backend: PATCH /api/applications/{id} accepts { follow_up_date: str (ISO date) | null } — null clears the reminder
Backend: GET /api/applications list projection includes follow_up_date
Backend: GET /api/applications/stats response adds follow_ups_due: int (count of non-archived applications where follow_up_date <= today and follow_up_date is not null)

Frontend: DetailPanel.jsx adds a 'Follow up' section below the stage selector: a date input (type='date') bound to follow_up_date, with a 'Clear' button to remove it; saved via useUpdateApplication on change (debounced 500ms)
Frontend: DetailPanel.jsx shows a yellow warning banner 'Follow-up overdue by {N} days' when follow_up_date is in the past

Frontend: create frontend/src/components/FollowUpBanner.jsx: a dismissable banner shown at the top of Dashboard (below OnboardingChecklist if present) when follow_ups_due > 0 — shows 'You have {N} follow-ups due' with a 'View' button that filters the application list to only show overdue follow-ups
ApplicationList.jsx: when follow_up_date is today or past, show a small bell icon in the row (between stage and date columns) with a tooltip 'Follow-up due {date}'
KanbanCard.jsx: show a small bell dot indicator on cards with overdue follow-ups
Backend: add follow_up_date as a sortable field — GET /api/applications?sort_by=follow_up_date sorts by soonest follow-up first (nulls last)
Backend test: test_applications.py adds test for PATCH with follow_up_date, verifies follow_ups_due count in stats, verifies sort by follow_up_date
Vitest test for FollowUpBanner: renders when follow_ups_due > 0, hidden when 0
All existing tests pass
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Follow-up reminders are a simple datetime field but high-impact for user outcomes. The 'follow-ups due' count in stats makes the dashboard feel alive and action-oriented. Can be extended later to include email/push notification when a follow-up is due.

userStories.title: Networking contacts tracker linked to applications
userStories.description: As a user, 70% of jobs come through networking but I have no way to track who I've talked to at each company. I want a lightweight contacts system where I can link people to applications and track when I last reached out.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend: create backend/contacts/ module with router.py, service.py, schemas.py following the standard split

Backend: contacts collection schema: { _id, user_id, name (required), company (optional), role (optional, their role), email (optional), linkedin_url (optional), notes (optional), relationship (enum: recruiter, referral, mentor, peer, hiring_manager, other), linked_applications: [ObjectId] (array of application _ids), last_contacted_at (datetime, optional), created_at, updated_at }
Backend: ensure indexes on contacts: (user_id, name) for lookup, (user_id, company) for company grouping
Backend: POST /api/contacts creates a contact (validate name required, relationship defaults to 'other') → 201

Backend: GET /api/contacts?company=X&relationship=Y lists user's contacts with optional filters, sorted by last_contacted_at desc (nulls last) — include pagination (cursor-based or limit/offset since contact counts are typically low)
Backend: GET /api/contacts/{id} returns single contact
Backend: PATCH /api/contacts/{id} updates fields (partial update), returns 200
Backend: DELETE /api/contacts/{id} deletes contact, returns 204
Backend: PATCH /api/contacts/{id}/link accepts { application_id: str } and adds it to linked_applications array (idempotent, no duplicates)
Backend: PATCH /api/contacts/{id}/unlink accepts { application_id: str } and removes it from linked_applications array
Backend: GET /api/contacts?application_id=X returns contacts linked to a specific application
Frontend: create frontend/src/api/contacts.js and frontend/src/hooks/useContacts.js following the existing api/hooks pattern

Frontend: DetailPanel.jsx adds a 'Contacts' section below calendar events: shows linked contacts as compact cards (name, role, relationship badge), with an 'Add Contact' inline form and a 'Link Existing' dropdown that searches existing contacts by name

Frontend: each contact card shows last_contacted_at as relative time ('3 days ago') with a 'Pinged' button that updates last_contacted_at to now (single click, PATCH with { last_contacted_at: new Date().toISOString() })
Frontend: contact cards show a stale indicator (amber dot) if last_contacted_at > 14 days ago
Backend test: test_contacts.py (new file) adds tests for CRUD, link/unlink, company filter, application_id filter
Vitest test: DetailPanel contacts section renders linked contacts, 'Pinged' button calls mutation
All existing tests pass
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This creates a new /contacts module following the exact router/service/schema pattern. Keep it lightweight — this is a CRM-lite, not Salesforce. The key UX is the 'Pinged' button (one-click to log a touchpoint) and the stale indicator. linked_applications is a simple array of ObjectIds, not a separate junction collection.

userStories.title: In-app notification center with bell icon and unread count
userStories.description: As a user, toasts disappear after a few seconds and I miss important updates. I want a persistent notification center in the NavBar that collects alerts about stale applications, upcoming interviews, goal completions, and follow-up reminders.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend: create backend/notifications/notification_service.py with functions: create_notification(user_id, type, title, body, action_url=None) -> str (returns notification_id), list_notifications(user_id, limit=50, unread_only=False) -> list[dict], mark_read(user_id, notification_id) -> bool, mark_all_read(user_id) -> int (returns count marked)

Backend: notifications collection schema: { _id, user_id, type (str: stale_app, interview_tomorrow, goal_reached, follow_up_due, weekly_digest), title, body, action_url (optional, relative URL like /dashboard?selected=xyz), read: bool (default false), created_at }
Backend: ensure index on notifications: (user_id, created_at desc), (user_id, read) for unread count

Backend: create backend/notifications/router.py: GET /api/notifications (list, default last 50), GET /api/notifications/unread-count (returns { data: { count: int } }), PATCH /api/notifications/{id}/read (marks one as read), PATCH /api/notifications/read-all (marks all as read)
Backend: mount notifications router at /api/notifications in main.py

Backend: in the APScheduler daily job (or add a new hourly job), generate notifications: (1) stale_app for each application not updated in 14+ days (max 5 per run, skip if already notified in last 7 days), (2) interview_tomorrow for calendar events happening tomorrow, (3) follow_up_due for overdue follow-up dates

Frontend: create frontend/src/api/notifications.js and frontend/src/hooks/useNotifications.js (useNotifications list query, useUnreadCount query that polls every 60 seconds via refetchInterval, useMarkRead mutation, useMarkAllRead mutation)

Frontend: create frontend/src/components/NotificationBell.jsx: a bell icon in NavBar with a red unread count badge (hidden when 0); clicking opens a dropdown panel (max-height 400px, scrollable) showing notifications sorted by newest first

Each notification item shows: colored icon by type (bell for follow-up, calendar for interview, chart for goal, clock for stale), title (bold), body (gray text), relative time, and is clickable (navigates to action_url if present, marks as read on click)
'Mark all as read' link at the top of the dropdown
Unread notifications have a blue left border or subtle blue background; read ones are plain
NotificationBell supports dark mode
Backend test: test_notifications.py (new file) adds tests for create, list, mark read, mark all read, unread count
Vitest test for NotificationBell: renders unread count badge, clicking notification marks it as read
All existing tests pass
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This creates the foundation for all future notification channels (email, push). The hourly APScheduler job generates notifications server-side; the frontend polls unread count every 60s (cheap query on indexed field). Notifications auto-expire — add a TTL index on created_at (30 days) so old notifications are cleaned up by MongoDB.

userStories.title: Activity feed with unified timeline of all actions
userStories.description: As a user reviewing my job search progress (or sharing with a mentor via US-077 public link), I want a chronological feed of all my actions: applications submitted, stage changes, interviews scheduled, notes added. This replaces mentally reconstructing 'what did I do this week?' from scattered data.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend: GET /api/activity?days=30 (default 30, max 365) returns a unified, chronological feed of user actions from the last N days

The activity feed is built from a single aggregation pipeline using $unionWith across collections: (1) applications: extract { type: 'applied', application_id, company, role_title, timestamp: date_applied }, (2) applications.stage_history: extract { type: 'stage_change', application_id, company, role_title, from_stage, to_stage, timestamp: changed_at } for each stage_history entry, (3) calendar_events: extract { type: 'event_created', application_id, event_type, title, company, timestamp: created_at }
Results sorted by timestamp desc, limited to 200 entries
Response format: { data: [{ type, timestamp, application_id, company, role_title, details: {...} }], meta: { total: int } }
Frontend: create frontend/src/pages/Activity.jsx: a new page at route /activity showing the activity feed as a vertical timeline (left border line with dots at each event)

Each activity entry renders differently by type: 'applied' shows 'Applied to {role} at {company}' with a blue dot, 'stage_change' shows 'Moved {company} {role} from {from_stage} to {to_stage}' with a purple dot, 'event_created' shows 'Scheduled {event_type} for {company}' with an orange dot
Clicking any entry navigates to /dashboard?selected={application_id} to open the relevant application
Activity page has a time range selector: 'Last 7 days', 'Last 30 days', 'Last 90 days', 'All time'
Add 'Activity' link to NavBar between Analytics and Job Board
App.jsx adds lazy-loaded route for /activity (protected)
Activity page supports dark mode
Backend test: test_activity.py (new file) creates applications + stage changes + events, verifies the activity endpoint returns them chronologically with correct types
Vitest test for Activity page: renders timeline entries, clicking entry navigates to dashboard
All existing tests pass
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: The $unionWith aggregation is the cleanest approach — no new collection needed, data is always fresh from source. The activity feed is read-only and purely derived. For stage_history, unwind the array to get individual entries. The timeline UI is a simple vertical line with positioned dots — no charting library needed, just Tailwind borders and positioning.

userStories.title: Extension auto-save mode for power users
userStories.description: As a power user who browses 50+ job pages per session, clicking 'Save' on every page is tedious. I want an auto-save toggle that automatically saves applications when I visit a job page, with a confirmation banner instead of a save button.
acceptanceCriteria: Extension: add an 'auto_save' setting to chrome.storage.local (default: false)
Extension: popup.js adds an 'Auto-save' toggle switch in the settings section of the popup (below the recent saves list) — toggling it updates chrome.storage.local.auto_save

Extension: content.js reads auto_save from chrome.storage.local on page load; if true AND isJobPage() AND extraction produced at least role_title or company_name, automatically trigger the save after a 2-second debounce (to allow page to fully render)
Extension: when auto-save triggers, the banner shows 'Saved to Pipelined' (green check icon) instead of the 'Save to Pipelined' button — auto-dismisses after 3 seconds
Extension: if auto-save results in a duplicate, the banner shows 'Already in your pipeline' (same as manual duplicate) and auto-dismisses after 3 seconds
Extension: if auto-save fails (network error, not authenticated), the banner shows 'Auto-save failed' with a manual 'Retry' button (does not auto-dismiss on error)

Extension: auto-save does NOT fire if the user has already manually saved on this page in this session (track via a Set of URLs in content.js module scope to prevent double-saves when navigating back)
Extension: auto-save debounce resets if the page URL changes (SPA navigation detection via MutationObserver on document.title or popstate listener, whichever is simpler for the existing architecture)
Extension: when auto_save is enabled, the popup toggle shows 'Auto-save: ON' in green text; when disabled, 'Auto-save: OFF' in gray

Extension test: test_content.js adds tests for auto-save flow: verifies save is triggered after debounce when auto_save=true, verifies save is NOT triggered when auto_save=false, verifies duplicate URL is not re-saved
Extension test: test_popup.js adds test for auto-save toggle rendering and state persistence
All existing extension tests still pass
npm test passes in /extension
userStories.notes: The 2-second debounce is important — some job boards (LinkedIn, Workday) update the DOM after initial load. The URL-based dedup Set prevents re-saving when the user navigates back to an already-saved page. Auto-save is opt-in and off by default to avoid surprising new users.

userStories.title: Extension LinkedIn profile contact save
userStories.description: As a user networking on LinkedIn, when I visit someone's LinkedIn profile, I want the extension to offer saving them as a contact in Pipelined, linked to applications at their company.
acceptanceCriteria: Extension: create extension/content/boards/linkedin_profile.js — a new board module with BOARD_ID='linkedin_profile' that matches LinkedIn profile pages (URL pattern: /linkedin\.com\/in\//)
isJobPage() returns true only if the URL matches a profile page (not /jobs/, /feed/, etc.) AND a profile name element is found in the DOM

extractFields() returns a DIFFERENT shape for profiles: { type: 'contact', name: string, headline: string (role/title), company: string (current company from experience), linkedin_url: window.location.href } — extract from profile DOM selectors (.text-heading-xlarge for name, .text-body-medium for headline)
Extension: content.js detects the 'contact' type return and renders a DIFFERENT banner: 'Save {name} as a contact?' with a 'Save Contact' button (blue) instead of 'Save to Pipelined'
Extension: service worker handles a new message type SAVE_CONTACT: POST /api/contacts with { name, role: headline, company, linkedin_url, relationship: 'other' } — requires contacts API from US-086
If the contact already exists (409 from backend or client-side check), banner shows 'Already in your contacts'
After saving, the banner shows the contact name with a check icon, auto-dismisses after 2 seconds
Extension: manifest.json content_scripts already includes linkedin.com/*, so no manifest change needed (profile pages are under the same origin)

Extension: content.js BOARDS array includes linkedin_profile AFTER the linkedin job parser, so job pages are matched first (job pages take priority over profile pages on URLs that could match both — but /in/ URLs won't match the job parser's /jobs/ pattern)
Extension test: test_linkedin_profile.js adds JSDOM fixture test for profile extraction (name, headline, company from DOM)
All existing extension tests still pass
npm test passes in /extension
userStories.notes: This depends on US-086 (contacts API) being implemented first. If US-086 is not done, the service worker save will fail gracefully with a 'Contacts feature coming soon' message. LinkedIn profile DOM changes frequently — use a fallback chain of 3+ selectors like the job parser does.

userStories.title: Application duplicate merge with conflict resolution
userStories.description: As a user who applies to the same company through multiple channels (extension + manual + CSV import), I sometimes end up with near-duplicate applications that differ slightly in fields. I want to merge two applications into one, choosing which fields to keep from each.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend: POST /api/applications/merge accepts { source_id: str, target_id: str } — merges source into target: for each field, keeps the target's value unless it's null/empty, in which case use source's value; stage_history arrays are concatenated and sorted by timestamp; calendar_events linked to source are re-linked to target; source application is then deleted
Backend: the merge endpoint returns the updated target application (200)
Backend: merge verifies both applications belong to the requesting user_id (404 if either not found)
Backend: merge is transactional (Motor session) — if any step fails, the entire merge rolls back
Frontend: ApplicationList.jsx adds a 'Merge' option in the bulk action bar when exactly 2 applications are selected — button is hidden when 0, 1, or 3+ selected

Clicking 'Merge' opens a frontend/src/components/MergeDialog.jsx modal: shows both applications side-by-side, for each field (company, role, stage, location, compensation, notes, tags) shows both values with radio buttons to pick which to keep (pre-selects the non-null or more recently updated value)
The merge dialog shows a 'Preview' of the resulting merged application at the bottom before confirming
Confirm button calls POST /api/applications/merge with the source_id being the application whose fields are mostly NOT selected, target_id being the one kept
After successful merge, React Query cache is invalidated for both applications and the list

Backend test: test_applications.py adds tests for merge: happy path merges fields correctly, stage_history is combined, calendar events are re-linked, source is deleted; merge with invalid IDs returns 404; merge across users returns 404
Vitest test for MergeDialog: renders both applications, radio buttons change preview, confirm calls merge API
All existing tests pass
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Duplicate applications are inevitable with multiple input channels (extension, manual, CSV import). The merge flow is the correct solution — not just deleting one, because each may have unique notes, tags, stage history, or linked events. The transactional merge prevents data loss. This is a power-user feature but critical for data hygiene.

userStories.title: Job board saved searches with new listing alerts
userStories.description: As a user browsing the job board, I want to save a search query (e.g., 'SWE intern remote') and be notified when new listings match my saved criteria, so I don't have to manually re-check the board every day.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend: create a saved_searches collection: { _id, user_id, name (string, user-chosen label), query (string), filters: { role_type, experience_level, remote_status, company_type, min_salary } (all optional), last_checked_at (datetime), new_matches_count (int, default 0), created_at }
Backend: POST /api/saved-searches creates a saved search → 201; max 10 per user (return 409 if limit reached)
Backend: GET /api/saved-searches lists user's saved searches
Backend: DELETE /api/saved-searches/{id} deletes a saved search → 204

Backend: GET /api/saved-searches/{id}/results runs the saved search's query+filters against job_listings and returns results (same format as GET /api/jobs), also resets new_matches_count to 0 and updates last_checked_at

Backend: in the GitHub sync job (after new listings are inserted), for each user's saved searches, count how many new listings (ingested_at > saved_search.last_checked_at) match the search criteria, and increment new_matches_count
Backend: the notification system (US-087, if available) creates a notification when new_matches_count > 0 after a sync: '{N} new jobs match your saved search "{name}"'
Frontend: JobBoard.jsx adds a 'Save this search' button next to the search/filter bar (visible when any search query or filter is active) — opens a small popover with a name input and 'Save' button
Frontend: JobBoard.jsx shows a 'Saved Searches' sidebar section (or tab) listing saved searches with name, filter summary, and new_matches_count badge (blue pill if > 0)
Clicking a saved search applies its query and filters to the job board
Frontend: create frontend/src/hooks/useSavedSearches.js with CRUD hooks following existing pattern
Backend test: test_saved_searches.py (new file) adds tests for CRUD, max 10 limit, results endpoint
Vitest test: saved search button renders when filters active, saved searches list renders with badges
All existing tests pass
npm test passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Saved searches transform the job board from passive browsing to active monitoring. The new_matches_count is incremented during the existing GitHub sync APScheduler job, so there's no new cron job needed. The 10-search limit prevents abuse and keeps the sync job fast.
